excerpt: Faith comprises confession, knowledge, belief, acceptance, acknowledging what Allah has sent down, obeying the Imām and accepting his words
source_work: Kirmani, Rahat al-ʿAql (attributed to Imām al-Ṣādiq)

excerpt: Wherever Allah mentions faith in the Qur'an, He also mentions righteous deeds; these two are interconnected

The heart's faith comprises confession, knowledge, belief, acceptance and acknowledgment of all that Allah has sent down, including the Book and the Prophet, and acquisition of knowledge of the limits (hudūd)
source_work: al-Kirmani or similar Ismaili philosophical commentary
source_location_hint: on the faith of the heart

excerpt: In Qur'an 2:143, Allah explicitly designates prayer as faith when He says 'Allah will not let your faith go to waste'
Death is good news (a sweet fragrance) for the believer; when the soul of My believer reaches the throat, he rejoices
If faith were uniform and admitted neither deficiency nor excess...the earliest believers to accept faith received their ranks according to their precedence
source_location_hint: Section on faith's degrees and precedence among believers

excerpt: To reject faith means either to disregard God's commandments, or to confess outwardly yet harbor inward denial...Faith rests upon the unity of belief, word, and deed
source_authority: Qadi al-Nuʿman (Ismaili jurist and theologian, 10th c.)
source_location_hint: On the conditions and integrity of faith

excerpt: God's promised reward for faith in the Hereafter is Paradise...disbelief—the worst of human traits—meets with God's threat of Hellfire
source_location_hint: On reward and punishment in relation to faith

excerpt: Inner faith entails heartfelt affirmation...Through this inward confirmation, the believer's inner faith becomes established. The deeper this confirmation, the loftier his faith's station
source_location_hint: On inward and outward faith and their distinctions

excerpt: The Prophet first commanded confession—the foremost condition of faith—without demanding detailed knowledge...Once people confessed, the stages of deepening confirmation commenced
source_location_hint: On the priority of faith over knowledge in spiritual development

excerpt: Knowledge is the conception the soul forms by apprehending a reality. When the soul confesses this conception through speech, affirms it in the heart, and embeds it within itself...this conception is called faith
source_location_hint: On the relationship between knowledge and faith

excerpt: Those whose hijabs were inferior gave birth to faith
source_location_hint: section on emanation of Islam and Iman

excerpt: The sum of the name of Muhammad is equal to the number of Islam and the sum of the name of Ali is equal to the number of Iman
The Messenger of God created the Islamic structure and the Commander of the Faithful created the structure of faith
source_location_hint: cosmological correspondence between Prophet and Imam
